articles.title: BoJ to hold rates steady in April - Nikkei
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan is expected to maintain the policy rate at 0.75% at its upcoming meeting on April 27–28, 2026. While inflationary pressures remain a concern for policymakers, they are prioritizing stability amid the significant geopolitical and economic uncertainty. articles.title: Gold struggles near daily low on firmer USD as traders keenly await US-Iran peace talks
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) sticks to intraday losses below the $4,800 mark through the first half of the European session on Tuesday, though it holds above a one-week low touched the previous day. Investors remain skeptical about a potential US-Iran agreement amid the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: BoJ to hold rate in April - Nikkei
articles.expand: According to a report from Nikkei, the Bank of Japan (BoJ) is expected to leave interest rates unchanged at 0.75% in the monetary policy announcement on April 28.

articles.title: GBP: Mixed labour market signals for BoE – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank’s Chief UK Economist Sanjay Raja assesses the latest United Kingdom (UK) labour market data as stronger on the surface but still fragile underneath. He notes the surprise drop in unemployment and softer wage growth, which may comfort the Bank of England (BoE).

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the main highlight was the UK jobs report . The data showed a meaningful drop in the unemployment rate, lower than expected job gains and an easing in wage growth (although the data was higher than forecasts). All in all, the data isn't urging for rate cuts, so the BoE is more likely to remain neutral. Ahea...

articles.title: ECB: Lagarde signals no near-term rate move – Danske Bank
articles.expand: Danske Research Team highlights that ECB President Christine Lagarde sees the economic fallout from the Iran war as not yet reaching the ECB’s adverse scenario.

articles.title: NZD: Stagflation risks weigh on kiwi – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur notes New Zealand inflation at 3.1% year-on-year, slightly above the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) target, and warns that higher energy prices could push it further. He sees a possible rate hike in late May, which might briefly support the New Zealand Dollar (NZD).
